newsSince: No new material news since June 9. The recovery print (Q4 FY26 sales $1.31B, +35% YoY; June-quarter guide ~$1.456B midpoint; data-center connectivity wins) was the early-May earnings already reflected in the prior case, and the DCS ~$500M guide/price hikes were June 1 items. The +2.9% move since June 9 is recovery-story momentum, not a fresh catalyst.

sources.label: Futurum: Microchip Q4 FY26 beats with data center wins

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-alpha lens: nothing new since June 9. DCS guide and price hikes already chased; +45% YTD leaves little residual alpha for a secondary name. TimePictra 12 is incremental. Stays a balanced-but-not-mispriced secondary residual-alpha holding.
verdicts.bull: DCS connectivity/power content compounding ~65% in 2026; tight supply and embedded breadth give durable AI-adjacent leverage.
verdicts.neutral: Solid recovery story but already well-covered and rerated; limited residual mispricing.
verdicts.bear: +45% YTD prices in the recovery; broad cyclical embedded exposure caps residual alpha.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Continued DCS revenue ramp and bookings recovery
verdicts.primaryRisk: Cyclical embedded demand wobble after a sharp rerate

verdicts.rankRationale: AI-node bottleneck lens: MCHP is indirect (analog/power/RF), not HBM/advanced-packaging/networking. No node-relevance news since June 9; TimePictra is timing software. Prior down-move already reflected indirect exposure post-run. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Power and timing content rides AI-server buildout; data-center connectivity wins broaden node adjacency.
verdicts.neutral: AI-adjacent but a step removed from the true node bottleneck.
verdicts.bear: Not a node choke-point; broad analog cyclicality dominates the story.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Indirect node exposure limits bottleneck alpha

verdicts.rankRationale: Co-packaged-optics lens: MCHP is peripheral (infra controls/timing content), not a primary CPO play. The June TimePictra 12 timing-software launch is sync/control, not CPO-relevant. No change to its peripheral position since June 9. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Timing/sync content sits adjacent to high-speed optical infrastructure buildout.
verdicts.neutral: Embedded/analog supplier with only tangential CPO linkage.
verdicts.bear: Not a co-packaged-optics alpha candidate; broad cyclical exposure.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Timing-network content attach in optical infrastructure
verdicts.primaryRisk: Peripheral to the CPO supply chain
